title: rubus timbal-lagravei proles quincyi
intro: Rubus timbal-lagravei proles quincyi, also known as Quincé, is a variety of the black raspberry plant native to France. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of ellagic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quincé include its potential to reduce the risk of cancer, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Quincé can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of benefits and potential applications.
